section.title: Grades
section.p: General PCC Grading Guidelines are found at .

paragraphs.title: Attendance (2%)
paragraphs.p: To further encourage you to attend class, a part of your grade comes from attendance. You will be able to see your attendance record in D2L. If you have to miss a class meeting, contact me as soon as possible to see if I can offer you a way to make up the missed attendance credit. The main reason to attend class is indirect though. In class we have discussions that help you understand the topics better, and you have opportunities to make progress on your homework while I and your classmates are available.

paragraphs.title: Online Homework (25%)
paragraphs.p: You have online homework for each section using . See for details. The due date for each assignment is posted in . Usually, more than one section will be due at the same time. You should start working on a homework assignment soon after the class that is associated with that section of the book. But always read the textbook section before beginning .

paragraphs.title: Worksheets (25%)
paragraphs.p: Each week there is a worksheet. You will usually begin these worksheets in class, where you are encouraged to collaborate in small groups. They can be posted in D2L Assignmens up until the coming Sunday evening at 10:00pm. Later than that, they can only be turned in physically at the start of the next class meeting (Monday or Tuesday).

paragraphs.title: Exams (16% + 16% + 16%)
paragraphs.p: There are three exams. Each exam takes place in class, unless there are special circumstances.

paragraphs.title: Exams Minimum
paragraphs.p: Important: In order to pass the course, there is an additional requirement that you must pass at least one of the three exams with a 70% or higher. If you do not pass any of the exams, then the highest possible letter grade that you can end with is a D, even if your overall average is above 70%.
In particular, if you do not pass the first two exams, then in order to pass the course you must pass the final exam.

Similarly, in order to pass the course with a B, you must at least pass two of the three exams with a 70% or higher. If passing MTH 95 with at least a B is important for you, and if you do not pass the first exam, then you must pass both of the remaining exams.

paragraphs.title: Grading Options
paragraphs.p:
Students are solely responsible for adding, dropping, or withdrawing from any class, as well as for selecting their desired grading option. Please review
PCC Registration Policy
for more information.

paragraphs.title: Letter Grades
paragraphs.p: Your letter grade will be assigned based on the weighted average of the items above.
Except as described above, you must pass at least one exam to complete the course with a C or higher. And you must pass at least two exams to complete the course with a B or higher.
